name: How much money can I get for slipping at work and missing weeks of pay?
description: 66-75% wages + medical; $20K+ possible—depends on injury proof.[1]

mainEntity.name: How much money can I get for slipping at work and missing weeks of pay?

acceptedAnswer.text: 66-75% wages + medical; $20K+ possible—depends on injury proof.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: **Workers' comp typically covers 66-75% of lost wages, medical bills, and disability benefits—no cap on medical.** Value depends on injury severity, like back surgery or permanent impairment.[1][2] No pain/suffering in comp, but third-party suits allow more. Factors: pre-injury wage, recovery time, future care. Settlements average $20K-$50K for moderate cases. Use a calculator or attorney for estimate based on your records.
